L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where a non-cryptographically-secure pseudo-random number generator is used to generate a fallback secret for signing authentication tokens when the relevant security configuration is not explicitly set. An attacker could predict or brute-force the generated secret, potentially enabling token forgery or authentication bypass.

L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where a non-cryptographically-secure pseudo-random number generator is used to generate a fallback secret for signing authentication tokens when the relevant security configuration is not explicitly set. An attacker could predict or brute-force the generated secret, potentially enabling token forgery or authentication bypass.

ground_truth.reason: The Kerberos authenticator uses ThreadLocalRandom (a non-cryptographically-secure PRNG) to generate a fallback cookie signature secret when the configuration is not explicitly set, allowing an attacker to predict or brute-force the secret and forge authentication tokens.
